paper_title: Sediment Sampling and Conditioning in Toulon Bay

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from Toulon Bay for subsequent analysis of chemical contamination and DNA extraction.

steps.description: A 50 cm-long and 10 cm-diameter sediment core was sampled in a PlexiGlass Ò tube with the help of divers in September 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core using divers and PlexiGlass tube

methods.description: Procedure for sectioning the sediment core under controlled conditions and preparing samples for further analysis.

steps.description: The core was sliced under nitrogen-enriched atmosphere using sectioning material previously sterilized with bleach and cleaned for trace metal sampling with trace metal grade nitric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: slice sediment core under nitrogen atmosphere with sterilized tools

steps.description: The core was sectioned every 1 cm from depths 0 to 20 cm and subsequently every 2 cm from depths 20 to 46 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: section sediment core at specified intervals

steps.description: Half of each sediment slice was immediately stored at −20 C and freeze-dried for a period of 72 h under a vacuum of 10 −5 atm with a Pilot PCCPLS15 freeze dryer (Cryotec) and then homogenized.

The other half (subsequently called 'frozen' sediments) was immediately rinsed with a sterile buffer (SB) composed of 10 mM EDTA, 50 mM TrisHCl and 50 mM Na 2 HPO 4 at pH 8 to remove contaminants such as humic substances and divalent cations.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse sediment with sterile buffer to remove contaminants

steps.description: After centrifugation at 8,000Âg, the pellet was stored at −20 C until DNA extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and store sediment pellet for DNA extraction
